query: EU AI Act Scientific Panel Advisory Forum members list announced June 2026

mako.raw:
{"query":"EU AI Act Scientific Panel Advisory Forum members list announced June 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1213,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/the-advisory-forum-what-is-it-and-how-does-it-work/","title":"The Advisory Forum: What Is It And How Does It Work? | EU Artificial Intelligence Act","content":"On 1 June 2026, the Commission announced the composition of the Forum, consisting of 174 members selected from more than 700 applications.
This website is built and maintained by the Future of Life Institute — the world’s oldest and largest nonprofit working for the responsible development of AI.
The scientific panel of independent experts (the Scientific Panel) is a body established under the EU AI Act to support the enforcement of rules on general-purpose AI (GPAI) models and systems.
This page aims to provide an overview of the EU AI Act's enforcement provisions relating to Chapter V, namely the provisions that impose obligations on providers of general-purpose AI (GPAI) models.
This is a guest post written by legal compliance professionals Øystein Endal, Andrea Vcric, Sidsel Nag, Nick Malter and Daylan Araz (see section about authors at the end), drawing on their experience from running or consulting businesses integrating AI."},{"url":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-scientific-panel","title":"AI Act Scientific Panel | Shaping Europe’s digital future","content":"The Scientific Panel is an expert advisory body established by the European Commission in accordance with Article 68 of the AI Act in conjunction with Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2025/454.
60 world-class independent AI experts have been recruited to join the AI Act Scientific Panel.
These leading technical experts will directly support the implementation and enforcement of the AI Act.
Join the Horizon Europe Cluster 4 info days to learn more about the topics of the 2027 Work Programme, including: Key digital technologies and quantum technologies, Emerging enabling technologies, Artificial intelligence and robotics, Next generation internet, Advanced computing and big data.
The Commission published its study assessing the policy opportunity and technical feasibility of introducing an EU-level registry of Text and Data Mining opt-out."},{"url":"https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/scientific-panel/","title":"The Scientific Panel of Independent Experts: What Is It And How Does It Work? | EU Artificial Intelligence Act","content":"The Scientific Panel consists of up to 60 independent experts serving two-year renewable terms, selected to ensure fair gender balance and geographical representation.
Members of the Scientific Panel were appointed on 1 June 2026.
On 1 June 2026, the Commission announced that 60 independent experts had been recruited and published the full list of members, marking the panel’s establishment."},{"url":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-advisory-forum","title":"AI Act Advisory Forum | Shaping Europe’s digital future","content":"The Advisory Forum has been established under the provisions of Article 67 of the AI Act.
The 174 Advisory Forum members have been selected from more than 700 applications, across civil society, academia, and industry, including Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) and start-ups."},{"url":"https://www.lexisnexis.com/en-gb/legal/news/commission-appoints-scientific-panel-advisory-forum-to-support-eu-ai-act-enforcement","title":"EU AI Act enforcement: Commission appoints expert Scientific Panel and Advisory Forum to advise AI Office and national authorities on GPAI systemic risks, model classification, evaluation, standardisa","content":"To bolster enforcement of the EU Artificial Intelligence (AI) Act, the European Commission has set up a Scientific Panel and an Advisory Forum.
This Panel consists of 60 independent specialists, bringing expertise in frontier AI, engineering, technical auditing, industrial and societal impact..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"EU AI Act Scientific Panel Advisory Forum members list announced June 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://cdt.org/insights/cdt-europes-ai-bulletin-june-2026","title":"CDT Europe’s AI Bulletin: June 2026 - Center for Democracy and Technology","content":"After various months of delays in the AI Act implementation, June saw major milestones in the constitution of oversight bodies and publication of guidance, as well as the publication of the European Union’s long-awaited tech sovereignty strategy. Each of these two bodies has different roles in the AI Act implementation ecosystem: while the Scientific Panel – with 60 members in total – is expected to act independently and play an active role in the oversight of General-Purpose AI (GPAI) models, the Advisory Forum – currently at 172 members – was conceived as a bipartisan forum of technical experts with balanced representation of commercial and non-commercial representation to provide targeted advice to the European Commission and the AI Board. The European Commission published its Tech Sovereignty Package, headlined by two policy documents – the Communication on European Tech Sovereignty and EU Open Source Strategy, and a Strategic Roadmap for Digitalisation and AI in Energy – and two legislative initiatives, the Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) and the Chips Act 2.0.","score":0.92995757,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://aigovernancebrief.org/weekly-ai-governance-brief-1-7-june-2026","title":"1–7 June 2026 - Weekly AI Governance Brief","content":"The Advisory Forum consists of 174 members selected from more than 700 applications. stakeholder input into AI Act. ISA, CEN, CENELEC and ETSI.","score":0.87807417,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://ellis.eu/news/10-ellis-researchers-appointed-to-the-ai-act-scientific-panel","title":"10 ELLIS Researchers Appointed to the AI Act Scientific Panel | European Laboratory for Learning and Intelligent Systems","content":"10 ELLIS Researchers Appointed to the European Commission’s AI Act Scientific Panel. # 10 ELLIS Researchers Appointed to the AI Act Scientific Panel. On 1 June 2026, the European Commission announced the appointment of the AI Act Scientific Panel. ELLIS congratulates ten researchers affiliated with the ELLIS network on their selection to serve on this panel as independent scientific experts supporting the implementation of the AI Act. The AI Act Scientific Panel brings together 60 world-leading independent experts on AI who will support the implementation and enforcement of the EU AI Act. The panel advises the European AI Office and national authorities on matters related to general-purpose AI (GPAI) models and systems, including systemic risks, model evaluation methodologies, model classification, and market surveillance activities. The appointment of ten ELLIS-affiliated researchers highlights the strength of the ELLIS network and the scientific caliber of its researchers.","score":0.8690161,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-scientific-panel","title":"AI Act Scientific Panel | Shaping Europe’s digital future","content":"[Skip to main content](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-scientific-panel#main-content). This panel is complimentary to the [Advisory Forum](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-advisory-forum), which provides broader stakeholder input to the Commission and the [AI Board](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-board) from across industry, civil society, and academia. [European AI Office](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/ai-office).","score":0.8565368,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/the-advisory-forum-what-is-it-and-how-does-it-work","title":"The Advisory Forum: What Is It And How Does It Work? | EU Artificial Intelligence Act","content":"# The Advisory Forum: What Is It And How Does It Work? The Advisory Forum (the **Forum**) is a general advisory body to the European Commission and the AI Board, established to provide technical expertise, advise them, and to contribute to their tasks under the EU AI Act. It sits within the Act’s broader governance architecture, as one of the two advisory bodies, in addition to the Scientific Panel of independent experts (established under Article 68). In this overview, we summarise the Forum’s legal basis, functions, membership, and working methods, and note the Commission’s appointment of the Forum on 1 June 2026. The Forum is established under Article 67 of the AI Act to provide technical expertise and to advise the AI Board and the Commission, contributing to their tasks under the Act. Its role is to contribute stakeholder input on the implementation and application of the Act. At the request of the AI Board or the Commission, it may prepare opinions, recommendations, and written contributions.","score":0.8095324,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.67,"request_id":"16dd6d75-cfda-4997-b6a9-ba3c341b023f"}
